title: clf
summary: Clear the contents of the current figure, selected figure handles, or all figures.

gpu_support.notes: `clf` clears plotting state only. It does not execute numeric GPU kernels.

description: `clf` clears the contents of the current figure while keeping the figure handle itself alive. RunMat also accepts explicit figure handles and the `'all'` selector to clear multiple figures. The compatibility keyword `'reset'` is currently parsed but behaves the same as plain `clf`.
behaviors: `clf('all')` clears every known figure and returns the number of figures that were cleared.
`clf(handle1, handle2, ...)` clears multiple figures and returns the number cleared when more than one handle is supplied.
`clf('reset')` is currently treated the same as plain `clf`.

examples.description: Clear the current figure after plotting
examples.output: % out is the cleared figure handle

examples.description: Clear a specific figure by handle

faqs.question: Does `clf` delete the figure handle itself?
faqs.answer: No. It clears the figure contents but keeps the figure handle selected and available.

faqs.question: What does `clf('reset')` do today?
faqs.answer: RunMat parses the keyword for compatibility, but the current implementation treats it the same as plain `clf`.

faqs.question: What does `clf('all')` return?
faqs.answer: It returns the number of figures that were cleared. When no figures exist, it returns `0`.

gpu_residency: `clf` only mutates plotting state and does not affect array residency.
gpu_behavior: `clf` performs no provider dispatch; it clears figures from the plotting registry and renderer state.
